definition: The joint condition of extreme rate divergence AND low FX vol is historically associated with elevated subsequent FX dislocation. In 18 qualifying months since 1990, 61% preceded a 20%+ G10 pair move within 12 months versus 11% unconditional (Fisher exact p<0.001). This condition is currently active (Q4 2025-Q1 2026).
evidence: n=18 months in 420-month sample; 95% CI [36%, 83%] — even lower bound is 3x unconditional
Two prior clustered episodes (late 1999-2000, mid 2006-early 2007) both preceded major FX dislocations
Mechanistically: extreme divergence incentivizes large carry positions, low vol compresses risk measures allowing higher leverage
open_questions: Does not identify which pair will move or when
Whether the signal retains power after controlling for other early warning indicators
